ucs.title: [LLM] Lotus Blossom / Bilbug Sagerunex backdoor C2 to legitimate cloud services from injected svchost.exe
ucs.description: Hunts the signature behaviour of the Sagerunex implant family (Cisco Talos, Mar 2025) used by Lotus Blossom (aka Bilbug / Spring Dragon / Raspberry Typhoon): the implant injects into svchost.exe and beacons to attacker-controlled Dropbox, Twitter (X) and Zimbra mail tenants for C2. Pairing the unusual svchost → public-cloud-API egress with Sagerunex-style service-registry persistence catches the actor's specific living-off-trusted-infrastructure tradecraft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.process_name="svchost.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="svchost.exe") AND (All_Traffic.dest_host="*dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*content.dropbox*" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="api.twitter.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="twitter.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="x.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*zimbra*" OR All_Traffic.url="*/oauth2/token*twitter*" OR All_Traffic.url="*/2/files/upload*") AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_host IN ("*.update.microsoft.com","*.windowsupdate.com","*.delivery.mp.microsoft.com")) by host All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.dest_host All_Traffic.process_name | rename All_Traffic.* as * | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*rundll32*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*regsvr32*")) by host Registry.registry_value_data Registry.registry_key_name | rename Registry.* as * | rename count as svc_persist_hits] | where svc_persist_hits >= 1 OR count > 5
ucs.kql:
let _ms_update_hosts = dynamic(["update.microsoft.com","windowsupdate.com","delivery.mp.microsoft.com","download.microsoft.com","sls.update.microsoft.com"]);
let _sagerunex_c2_terms = dynamic(["dropboxapi.com","content.dropbox","api.twitter.com","twitter.com","x.com/i/api","zimbra"]);
let SagerunexEgress =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "svchost.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (_sagerunex_c2_terms)
    | where not(RemoteUrl has_any (_ms_update_hosts))
    | summarize Beacons = count(),
                FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
                LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
                Hosts     = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25),
                IPs       = make_set(RemoteIP, 25),
                ParentCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
    | where Beacons >= 3;
let SagerunexService =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath" or RegistryValueName =~ "ServiceDll"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\", "rundll32", "regsvr32")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","services.exe")
    | summarize PersistKey = any(RegistryKey),
                PersistVal = any(RegistryValueData),
                PersistTime = min(Timestamp)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName;
SagerunexEgress
| join kind=leftouter SagerunexService on DeviceId
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, ParentCmd,
          Beacons, Hosts, IPs,
          PersistKey, PersistVal, PersistTime
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _ms_update_hosts = dynamic(["update.microsoft.com","windowsupdate.com","delivery.mp.microsoft.com","download.microsoft.com"]);
let _sagerunex_c2_terms = dynamic(["dropboxapi.com","content.dropbox","api.twitter.com","twitter.com","x.com/i/api","zimbra"]);
let SagerunexEgress =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where SourceProcessName has "svchost.exe" or DeviceCustomString1 has "svchost.exe" or RequestClientApplication has "svchost"
    | where isnotempty(RequestURL) or isnotempty(DestinationHostName)
    | extend Target = coalesce(DestinationHostName, RequestURL, DestinationDnsDomain)
    | where Target has_any (_sagerunex_c2_terms)
    | where not(Target has_any (_ms_update_hosts))
    | summarize Beacons = count(),
                FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
                Targets   = make_set(Target, 25),
                DstIPs    = make_set(DestinationIP, 25)
                by SourceHostName, SourceIP
    | where Beacons >= 3;
let SagerunexService =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 4697 or EventID == 7045
    | extend SvcImage = coalesce(column_ifexists("ServiceFileName", ""), tostring(EventData))
    | where SvcImage has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\", "rundll32", "regsvr32")
    | summarize PersistTime = min(TimeGenerated), PersistVal = any(SvcImage) by Computer;
SagerunexEgress
| join kind=leftouter SagerunexService on $left.SourceHostName == $right.Computer
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, SourceHostName, Beacons, Targets, DstIPs, PersistVal, PersistTime
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lotus Blossom Sagerunex svchost C2 to Dropbox/Twitter/Zimbra
id: 8e6b3a2c-9f1d-4d9d-9f7a-1c0d4f5b6a91
status: experimental
description: Detects Sagerunex implant beaconing pattern used by Lotus Blossom (Bilbug, Spring Dragon, Raspberry Typhoon) — svchost.exe egressing to Dropbox API, Twitter/X API, or Zimbra-hosted C2 channels.
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/lotus-blossom-espionage-group/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0030/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2025/03/05
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\svchost.exe'
  selection_dest:
    DestinationHostname|contains:
      - 'dropboxapi.com'
      - 'content.dropbox'
      - 'api.twitter.com'
      - 'zimbra'
  filter_legit:
    DestinationHostname|contains:
      - 'update.microsoft.com'
      - 'windowsupdate.com'
      - 'delivery.mp.microsoft.com'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_dest and not filter_legit
fields:
  - Image
  - DestinationHostname
  - DestinationIp
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Genuine corporate Dropbox/Twitter integrations performed via svchost are extremely rare; verify against known third-party drivers/services.
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g0030
ucs.rationale: Sagerunex (a.k.a. Billbug/Bilbug second-stage backdoor evolved from Elise/Evora) is uniquely attributed to Lotus Blossom by Cisco Talos (Mar 2025) and Symantec/Broadcom (2022). Its identifying tradecraft is (a) injection into svchost.exe and (b) C2 over legitimate Dropbox, Twitter and Zimbra services — abuse Microsoft and other APTs do not commonly mimic. Combining the unusual svchost-to-cloud-API

ucs.title: [LLM] Lotus Blossom on-host recon burst followed by WinRAR staging (Bilbug discovery → collection chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the multi-step host-recon → archive-staging chain that Symantec (Bilbug 2022) and Cisco Talos (2025) observed Lotus Blossom operators executing manually after Sagerunex/Hannotog drops a foothold: a tight cluster of net.exe / netstat / ipconfig / tasklist / systeminfo / arp / whoami / nltest from one user, followed within an hour by WinRAR (rar.exe / WinRAR.exe) archiving into ProgramData or Users\Public.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as recon_bins dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_recon_bins min(_time) as recon_first max(_time) as recon_last from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","arp.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","route.exe","qwinsta.exe")) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE","*$") by Processes.user host _time | bin _time span=15m | stats sum(count) as recon_cmd_count values(recon_bins) as recon_bins values(distinct_recon_bins) as distinct_recon_bins min(recon_first) as recon_first max(recon_last) as recon_last by host Processes.user _time | rename Processes.user as user | where distinct_recon_bins >= 5 | join type=inner host user [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as rar_cmds values(Processes.process_name) as rar_bin min(_time) as archive_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","WinRAR.exe","7z.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*rar* a *","*7z* a *")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","* -hp*","* -p*")) by host Processes.user] | where archive_time >= recon_first AND archive_time <= (recon_last + 3600) | table recon_first recon_last archive_time host user distinct_recon_bins recon_bins rar_bin rar_cmds
ucs.kql:
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","arp.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","route.exe","qwinsta.exe"]);
let _archive_bins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let ReconBurst =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    | where FileName in~ (_recon_bins)
    | summarize ReconCount = count(),
                DistinctRecon = dcount(FileName),
                ReconBins = make_set(FileName, 20),
                SampleCmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
                ReconStart = min(Timestamp),
                ReconEnd   = max(Timestamp)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 15m)
    | where DistinctRecon >= 5;          // tight burst of >= 5 distinct discovery binaries in 15m
let RarStaging =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ (_archive_bins)
       or (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_archive_bins))
       or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(rar|7z)\b.*\s+a\s+"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", " -hp", " -p", " -m5", " -v")
    | project ArchiveTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ArchiveCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ArchiveBin = FileName;
ReconBurst
| join kind=inner RarStaging on DeviceId, AccountName
| where ArchiveTime between (ReconStart .. ReconEnd + 1h)
| extend DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', ArchiveTime, ReconStart)
| project ReconStart, ReconEnd, ArchiveTime, DelayMin,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          DistinctRecon, ReconBins, SampleCmds,
          ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd
| order by ReconStart desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","arp.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","route.exe","qwinsta.exe"]);
let _archive_bins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let ReconBurst =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where AccountType == "User"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in (_recon_bins)
    | summarize ReconCount = count(),
                DistinctRecon = dcount(Bin),
                ReconBins = make_set(Bin, 20),
                SampleCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
                ReconStart = min(TimeGenerated),
                ReconEnd   = max(TimeGenerated)
                by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
    | where DistinctRecon >= 5;
let RarStaging =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in (_archive_bins) or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(rar|7z)\b.*\s+a\s+"
    | where CommandLine has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", " -hp", " -p", " -m5", " -v")
    | project ArchiveTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ArchiveCmd = CommandLine, ArchiveBin = Bin;
ReconBurst
| join kind=inner RarStaging on Computer, Account
| where ArchiveTime between (ReconStart .. ReconEnd + 1h)
| extend DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', ArchiveTime, ReconStart)
| project ReconStart, ReconEnd, ArchiveTime, DelayMin,
          Computer, Account, DistinctRecon, ReconBins, SampleCmds,
          ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd
| order by ReconStart desc
ucs.rationale: Symantec's 2022 Bilbug report and Talos's 2025 Lotus Blossom report both call out the same hands-on-keyboard recon-then-RAR pattern as a Lotus Blossom signature: operators run a tight burst of native discovery commands (net/netstat/ipconfig/tasklist/systeminfo/arp/whoami/nltest) and then archive findings with WinRAR (often password-protected with -hp) into ProgramData or Users\Public for later exf
